pages.text:
Consorntorealty of Austratis.

WHEREAS the Executive Committee of the International

Institute of Refrigeration at its meetings held on the sixteenth day of October, one thousand nine hundred and fifty-two and twenty-fourth day of November, one thousand nine hundred and fifty-three, drew up an Agreement concern- ing the International Institute of Refrigeration to replace the International Convention of the twenty-first day of

June, one thousand nine hundred and twenty for the creation of the said Institute and the International Agreenent of the thirty-first day of Way, one thousand nine hundred and thirty-seven modifying the said Convention;

AND WHEREAS the Government of the Commonwealth of

Australia desires to sign the said Agreement, which is shortly to be opened for signature at Paris;

TOW THEREFORE these presents are to certify that Sit 'rank Keith Officer, an officer of the cavit Division of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire, upon whom has been conferred the Decoration of the Military Cross,

Her Majesty's Australian Ambassador Ixtraordinary and

Plenipotentiary to the Republic of France, has been duly named, constituted and appointed by the Government of the

Commonwealth of Australia as its Plenipotentiary and

Representative having Full Power and Authority to sign the said Agreement on its behalf.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF I, Philip Albert Martin MeBRIDE,

Acting Minister of State for External Affairs for the

Commonwealth of Australia, have hereunto set my hand and

DONE at Canberra this seventh day. of July one thousand nine hunäred and fifty-four.

Acting Minister of State for External Affairs
